sources.name: Cold local content-hash scan is single-threaded serial
sources.where: src/hpc_agent/infra/transport/_delta.py:220-266 (_build_local_manifest_cached, sequential loop over paths calling _sha256_of)
sources.mechanism: When the (size,mtime) cache misses — first push, cache-schema bump, .hpc wipe, or genuinely changed files — each file is streamed-hashed sequentially in one thread. hashlib releases the GIL and the work is IO+CPU parallelizable.
sources.cost: MEASURED: 39,374 files / 9.9 GB = ~37 min cold (_delta.py:109, the live run-13 number). A 4-8-way ThreadPool hash typically yields 3-6x on NTFS/SSD → ~6-10 min; changed-file-heavy re-pushes scale likewise.
sources.trigger: First push of a tree; any cache invalidation (version bump of _HASH_CACHE_VERSION, deleted .hpc); large changed sets.
sources.fix: Hash cache-miss files on a small ThreadPoolExecutor inside _build_local_manifest_cached (order-independent — entries are sorted after), keeping the cache write identical. The cache already eliminates the re-push case; this fixes the unavoidable-cold case.

sources.name: Three-to-four full UNPRUNED tree walks per submit (rglob descends into excluded dirs; S1 and S2 each re-walk)
sources.where: src/hpc_agent/infra/transport/_disclose.py:106 & 161 (deploy_payload_summary / _disclose_payload root.rglob('*')), src/hpc_agent/infra/transport/_excludes.py:206 (_pushable_relpaths rglob), src/hpc_agent/ops/submit_blocks.py:448-468 (_deploy_payload_brief at S1)
sources.mechanism: Per run: S1's resolved brief walks the tree (deploy_payload_summary), then the S2 push walks it again for the ship-size disclosure (_disclose_payload), again for the delta file set (_pushable_relpaths), then stats every file a fourth time in _build_local_manifest_cached. All three walkers use Path.rglob('*') which ENUMERATES every entry inside excluded directories (.git, .venv, results/, _per_task_results/, _dossier/) and only then filters via _path_excluded — per-path fnmatch over ~20 patterns. The remote snippet prunes dirs (dirs[:] = ...); the local walkers do not. The 50k walk cap counts only INCLUDED files, so a huge excluded .venv/results mirror is still fully enumerated.
sources.cost: Estimate: a 39k-pushable-file experiment tree with a .venv + results mirrors is easily 100-300k directory entries; NTFS enumeration+stat at ~5-15k entries/s → ~10-40s per walk, x3-4 walks = ~30-160s per submit of duplicated filesystem scanning, before any hashing. (The run-#12 finding-3 'scan read as a hang twice in one night' is the observed symptom class.)
sources.trigger: Every S1-resolve (brief disclosure) and every S2 push; worst with large excluded subtrees (venvs, pulled result mirrors) which the exclusion should make FREE but the walk still pays.
sources.fix: One shared pruned walker in _excludes (os.walk with dirs[:] filtering via _path_excluded on the directory prefix — the remote snippet's exact shape) returning (relpath, size) pairs; deploy_payload_summary, _disclose_payload, and _pushable_relpaths all consume it, and rsync_push computes the payload total from the SAME result it hands the manifest builder (sizes are already collected) — one walk per push, and directory pruning makes excluded subtrees O(1).

sources.name: 5-6 separate cold SSH round-trips per S2 push even when zero bytes ship (no ControlMaster on native Windows)
sources.where: src/hpc_agent/ops/submit_flow.py:166-217 (_preflight_probe), transport/_delta.py:323 (_remote_push_manifest), transport/_prune.py:51-131 (_read_prior_push_manifest + _write_push_manifest, plus one checkpoint write per 256MB/2000-file batch), transport/__init__.py:1216-1304 (deploy_runtime prelude + manifest write), submit_flow.py:560-589 (executor existence probe)
sources.mechanism: The S2 prelude issues each remote operation as its own ssh exec: (1) probe 'true', (2) remote hash manifest, (3) prune's 'cat .push_manifest' when extras exist, (4) final _write_push_manifest (+1 checkpoint write per delta batch — 4 extra for the 1181MB/5-batch push), (5) deploy_runtime's mkdir/find/cat prelude, (6) 'test -f' per distinct executor, (7) deploy-manifest write when changed. On native Windows ControlMaster is broken (ssh_options demotes to ControlMaster=no), so every exec is a FULL cold handshake; hoffman2's evening MaxStartups throttle punishes exactly this pattern (the root cause of the run #7-#8 stalls).
sources.cost: Estimate: 2-5s per cold handshake over the VPN (observed range; ~31s under hoffman2 login-node load per the run-#7 probe post-mortem) x 5-6 execs = ~15-30s per S2 on a healthy link even with an unchanged tree, and minutes under throttle. The 5-batch push adds 4 checkpoint writes ≈ 10-20s more.
sources.trigger: Every S2 push; amplified on throttled/loaded login nodes and whenever the delta batches (each batch = transfer + checkpoint exec).
sources.fix: Fold round-trips: (a) ONE combined pre-push exec that does the probe, deploy_runtime's mkdir/prep, and cats BOTH manifests (.push_manifest + .deploy_state) while running the hash snippet — everything is read-only and independent, exactly like deploy_runtime already folds its cat into the mkdir prelude; (b) append the push-manifest write (and per-batch checkpoint) to the SAME remote command as the batch's 'tar x' extract leg (base64 doc after extraction, same connection); (c) fold the executor 'test -f' into the stage-swap/extract command. Net: 2 round-trips per push (prelude + transfer) instead of 5-10.

sources.name: submit-preflight re-runs install-commands + full fan-out on every S1 invocation (S1 runs twice per submit)
sources.where: src/hpc_agent/ops/submit_preflight.py:180-253, src/hpc_agent/_wire/workflows/submit_blocks.py:162-168 (run_preflight default True), src/hpc_agent/ops/submit_blocks.py:474-477
sources.mechanism: SubmitS1Spec.run_preflight defaults True, and the driver invokes S1 twice per run (pre-resolve boundary, then with the resolve spec) — each time spawning install-commands (asset copy into ~/.claude, NOT in VERB_MODULE_MAP so it also pays the 7.2s full registry walk), load-context, check-preflight (a cold cluster ssh probe), and resolve-resources as parallel subprocesses. No verdict TTL exists (unlike the uv preflight's #255 cache), so the identical environment is re-proven minutes apart; S2's own _preflight_probe then probes the SAME cluster a third time.
sources.cost: Wall-clock per S1 ≈ max(parallel arms) = the ssh check-preflight arm (~5-15s cold, more under throttle) or install-commands (~7.2s startup + asset IO); x2 S1 invocations ≈ 15-30s per run of re-proving an unchanged machine+cluster, plus the duplicate S2 probe.
sources.trigger: Every submit run with default specs (both S1 calls); every re-submit in the same session.
sources.fix: TTL-cache the submit-preflight verdict keyed on (cluster, framework version, clusters.yaml mtime) mirroring the existing preflight_cache (#255) so the second S1 and S2's probe reuse the first probe's verdict within the window; drop install-commands from the per-submit path entirely (content-hash skip, or move it to setup/doctor — its assets change only on package upgrade, which the deploy cache already keys on).

census.notes: Scope covered: CLI/MCP dispatch (dispatch.py fast path, _verb_module_map, plugin gate — measured 7.2s full walk vs 1.3-1.8s fast path vs 1.13s import floor, load_plugins 0.35s/process), S1 resolve (resolve_submit_inputs is purely local — its interview.json/sidecar re-reads are sub-second and were deliberately NOT reported per the <1s rule; the latency lives in the submit-preflight fan-out), and S2 staging residual (transport/__init__.py, _delta.py, _disclose.py, _excludes.py, _prune.py, submit_flow prelude). Confirmed NOT re-found: the push-hash cache, batched ship + per-batch checkpointing (though the checkpoint's per-batch ssh cost is folded into the round-trip finding as a residual), pull-mirror excludes, [fatal] disclosure. Biggest residual levers by expected wall-clock: (1) remote full-tree re-hash per push (est. 1-5 min/push — the direct remote twin of the already-fixed local finding 6), (2) uncompressed tar|ssh on the 2 MB/s VPN (~5-7 min avoidable on the measured 1181 MB push), (3) the 3-4 duplicate unpruned tree walks (~30-160s/submit), (4) cold-SSH round-trip fan (~15-30s/push, minutes under hoffman2 throttle), (5) dispatch overhead multiplied by the block-drive subprocess-per-span design. Caveat: the dev venv here has NO plugin installed, so the register_cli fast-path kill is asserted conditionally — verify in the demo/hook envs (where hpc-agent-notebook-render is installed) whether its entry point exposes register_cli; if it does, every live verb call is at the 7.2s rate, making finding 1 the largest per-call multiplier in the stage. Out-of-scope items honored: no scheduler queue-wait findings; canary poll cadence (S2's detached poll loop) sits in the S2-worker/monitor stage, not audited here.

ranked.name: Ship the -canary2 sidecar + escalate deterministic reporter errors (sidecar_not_found/rc=2) instead of polling the 1800s budget
ranked.cost: MEASURED ~30 min per gated submit (run-13: 22:42:28Z submit -> 23:12:43Z verdict while the job finished in 97s); fires on EVERY double-canary S2 today because fire_second_canary mints the sidecar post-deploy and nothing pushes it
ranked.fix: (a) mint/ship ALL canary sidecars pre-deploy (or one <1KB scp in fire_second_canary); (b) treat the reporter's structured deterministic errors (sidecar_not_found, sidecar_parse_error, tasks_py_not_found — any rc) as the existing 3-poll escalation class in _classify_poll_failure, especially once the scheduler shows the job terminal

ranked.name: Pull engine parity: server-side filtered tar pull with delta + batched durable checkpoints (replaces _scp_pull whole-tree, folds trace pull into the metrics pull)
ranked.cost: MEASURED 40-90 min double-pulls (run-12), 1800s flat-timeout kills with zero-byte restarts, and the trace-ingest pull doubles it (second full pull of the same subtree on the scp path); filter honored = KB instead of 1.18 GB
ranked.fix: `ssh 'find <subdir> -name <pattern> | tar c -T -' | tar x` pull analogue of _tar_ssh_push: remote find honors the include list (the 1000x lever), content-hash delta against local files, bounded batches landing directly in the destination (retry pulls only the remainder), stall-based deadline instead of the flat 1800s; pass include=[summary_name, TRACE_TRANSPORT_FILENAME] so traces ride the one pull

ranked.name: Flip HPC_SSH_ENGINE=asyncssh to default (post-run-13 live validation) — one persistent channel per host
ranked.stage: E-ssh-substrate (E2; subsumes E1 cold-dial, E7 slot contention, E8 Windows pipe stall, E11 safe_interval, F3 breaker trips)
ranked.cost: ~2-6 min pure handshake per run (70-130 execs x 1-3s, ControlMaster broken on native Windows) + MEASURED ~9 min breaker dead-wait per worker when the post-transfer connection burst trips it (a4b00fdb lines 145-161) + the F-N 15-min slot-wait class; predecessor broker measured ~150x on the command channel
ranked.fix: Default the built-and-hardened asyncssh engine on with 'native' as opt-out (reaper F56 and dispatched-guard F55 already fixed; hard fallback to one-shot guarantees never-worse). Deletes per-exec dials, moots the throttle, lets 8 sessions share one slot, and removes the self-inflicted breaker storms

ranked.name: Preamble-free lightweight liveness poll: qstat/squeue until the job leaves the queue, full reporter exactly once at terminal
ranked.stage: B/C/E/F merged (B5+E6+F4+F7 — one root cause: conda-activation + 2 python starts + full task walk per poll)
ranked.cost: MEASURED: 60s-timeout x3 + 300s breaker = ~490s dead per s3 worker (1af806fe); ~45-60 min of fork-exhausted dead watches over 11h (run-12); healthy-path ~2-6s remote per poll x 40-80 watch polls = 2-10 min/run of re-done remote compute, and slow polls feed false connection-failures to the breaker
ranked.fix: Poll scheduler state via the already-built ssh_batch_scheduler_states (no python, no login shell, <0.5s) while queued/running; read sidecars with bare python3/cat (no module load, no conda); run the heavyweight reporter once when the job leaves the queue; account command-timeouts separately from connect-failures in breaker math; persist reporter-unreachable per (host, env-fingerprint) so relaunched watches fail fast

ranked.name: Remote-side push-manifest cache: stop re-sha256ing the entire remote tree every delta push
ranked.cost: MEASURED ~700-740s of a single ssh.exe child per S2 push (a4b00fdb hb 120s->812s) at run-13 tree scale; the local twin of this cache landed (finding 6), the remote side did not; smaller post-_aggregated-exclude but still minutes per push
ranked.fix: Persist (path, size, mtime_ns, sha256) in the .hpc/.push_manifest.json the push already writes; the remote snippet re-hashes only files whose (size, mtime_ns) moved; absent manifest falls back to the full hash

ranked.name: Dispatcher creates the announce dir eagerly at run start — kill the pre-first-marker full reporter-walk fallback
ranked.cost: MEASURED 20-25 min per reporter walk on run-12 fleets (cited in-code, announce.py:10-12); every monitor tick between submit and the first terminal marker pays it — potentially 2-3 consecutive walks during queue wait producing zero information
ranked.fix: Create .hpc/announce/<run_id>/ (optionally .started markers) at dispatch time so the one-readdir census leg owns the lifecycle from tick 1; F17 scheduler-liveness probe already answers 'is anything alive' without a walk; walk fallback decays to legacy runs only

ranked.name: Compress the tar|ssh push stream by default on the rsync-less (Windows/VPN) path
ranked.cost: MEASURED: 1181 MB at ~2 MB/s = ~10 min per big push; ~5-7 min avoidable at typical 2-4x compression of py/JSON/CSV payloads; the Compression=no pin assumes a fast LAN the live link is not
ranked.fix: tar cz (or --zstd with plain-tar fallback probe) on the tar|ssh legs, symmetric tar xz remote; keep the fast-LAN opt-out as the env knob instead of the current hidden HPC_SSH_COMPRESSION=yes opt-in

ranked.name: Parallelize the double canary: fire both qsubs at Phase-1 submit, verify both
ranked.cost: +1 full (queue wait + 97s run + detect + verify) cycle serialized into every fresh gated submit today ≈ 3-10 min; nothing in the n=2 diff requires ordering
ranked.fix: Submit both canary jobs back-to-back with both sidecars minted+shipped pre-deploy (rank-1's fix provides this), let the scheduler run them concurrently, verify both — canary stage = max() of two jobs instead of sum

ranked.name: Flip cluster-final reduce (#254) to default — pull one KB metrics_aggregate.json instead of every wave partial
ranked.cost: Tens of seconds to minutes per harvest re-pulling hundreds of wave partials with no delta/filter on the scp path, vs ~5-10s on the built path (verified opt-in at aggregate_flow.py:1573; output documented byte-identical)
ranked.fix: Use cluster-final reduce whenever the combiner is deployed and activation resolves, existing local-reduce fallback on failure; HPC_CLUSTER_FINAL_REDUCE becomes the opt-OUT debug knob

ranked.name: Honor the canary TTL cache (#249) on the gated submit-s2 path (needs maintainer ruling — documented design comment)
ranked.cost: Full canary stage (2x queue+run+detect+verify ≈ 5-20 min) re-paid on every re-submit of an already-validated cmd_sha inside the 4h TTL; the cache is structurally dead on the standard path while S3's gate already trusts it
ranked.fix: submit_and_verify checks is_canary_validated_fresh before Phase 1; on hit return verified=True with the existing cache-hit CAUTION disclosure. Requires a ruling: the bypass is commented as intentional ('explicit request to validate') but 100%-dead-on-standard-path reads as drift

ranked.name: Run-scope the pull_summaries pull (finding-19 fix never applied to this surface)
ranked.stage: D-harvest-aggregate (D4) — VERIFIED: _run_scoped_results_subdir used only at :413/:576, not :1651
ranked.cost: Pulls the entire shared results/ root (every prior run's outputs) on every pull_summaries=true aggregate; the identical unscoped shape measured as an 1800s timeout in run-12 finding 19; grows with campaign history
ranked.fix: One-liner: remote_subdir=_run_scoped_results_subdir(experiment_dir, run_id, record, results_subdir) at aggregate_flow.py:1651, same call the two sibling pulls make

ranked.name: In-process dispatch for same-package subprocess children (block-drive spans, submit-preflight and aggregate-preflight fan-outs)
ranked.cost: MEASURED floors: 1.13s import, 1.3-1.8s fast-path verb, 7.2s full-path, 5.36s `hpc-agent capabilities`; ~6 child processes per S1+tick ≈ 8-12s at fast-path rates, 30-45s at full-path; multiplied across every tick of every run
ranked.fix: Invoke the registered primitive (meta.func) directly in-process for short same-package verbs; keep the subprocess seam only for watch-class verbs needing deadline-kill/bounded-capture isolation

ranked.name: Fast-path granularity: per-verb plugin manifest, map the unmapped verbs, cache the plugins-present verdict
ranked.cost: MEASURED 7.2s full-registry walk vs 1.3-1.8s fast path per invocation; any register_cli plugin (the live demo env's notebook-render) disqualifies EVERY verb; load_plugins entry-point scan 0.35s per process unconditionally; ~55s per 10-verb run at full-path rates
ranked.fix: Plugin MANIFEST declares which core verbs it reshapes so add-only plugins keep the fast path; add install-commands/grouped verbs to VERB_MODULE_MAP; cache the plugins verdict keyed on the installed-distribution set
depends_on: rank 12 reduces how often this is paid (fewer subprocesses), but the MCP/CLI entry itself still needs it

ranked.name: submit-preflight verdict TTL + gate install-commands on a version stamp
ranked.stage: A-dispatch-s1-s2 (A8, install-commands half shared with D8)
ranked.cost: ~15-30s per run re-proving an unchanged machine+cluster (S1 runs twice, each fanning install-commands + cold ssh check-preflight; S2 probes the same cluster a third time); install-commands rewrites ~/.claude on every submit AND every aggregate
ranked.fix: TTL-cache the verdict keyed on (cluster, framework version, clusters.yaml mtime) mirroring #255's preflight_cache; skip install-commands unless the wheel stamp changed

ranked.name: One shared pruned tree walker for the 3-4 per-submit local walks (rglob descends excluded dirs)
ranked.cost: ~30-160s per submit of duplicated NTFS enumeration; rglob('*') enumerates inside .git/.venv/results mirrors before filtering, and S1 brief + S2 disclosure + delta set + manifest stat each re-walk
ranked.fix: os.walk with dirs[:] pruning via _path_excluded (the remote snippet's exact shape) in _excludes, returning (relpath, size); all three consumers plus the manifest builder share one walk per push

ranked.name: Batch wave combines: one remote exec for all newly-complete/missing waves (monitor tick and aggregate _combine_missing)
ranked.cost: W separate cold dials: 10 waves in one tick ≈ 40-60s; a 20-wave never-combined aggregate ≈ 1.5-3 min, 100-wave ≈ 5-12 min of pure connection+interpreter overhead
ranked.fix: Extend the deployed combiner with --waves N,M,... (or --all-missing) emitting per-wave ok/fail JSON; both call sites journal from the one result. Longer-term: dispatcher combines cluster-side when a wave's last task announces (rank 9 subsumes the final reduce)

ranked.name: Deterministic-failure memo for aggregate re-runs (same spec + same remote tree fingerprint => cached verdict) + remote pre-pull integrity check
ranked.cost: MEASURED: byte-identical failed attempts 89 and 61 min apart, each redoing the full pull (>=1800s); run-12 aggregate night ~5.7h for 5 attempts, 2 pure repeats
ranked.fix: Memo keyed on (resolved input spec, remote tree fingerprint) returns the cached verdict as needs-decision instantly; run the cheap foreign-rows count remotely BEFORE pulling so the integrity check costs one round-trip, not a mirror pull
depends_on: rank 2 shrinks the re-paid work; memo prevents the repeat entirely

ranked.name: Fold the post-terminal canary verify tail + S2/S3 pre-submit probes into single compound remote scripts
ranked.cost: Verify tail: 6-12 round trips x 1.5-4s ≈ 10-45s per gated submit; submit chain: 3-5 collapsible trips ≈ 8-20s, paid at S2 and S3; both largely handshake cost
ranked.fix: One sentinel-sectioned bash -c per boundary: tail + cat _runtime.json + checkpoint probe + sha in the verify tail; test -f executor && mkdir -p logdir && uv probe folded into the deploy/submit round trip; collapse the two metrics-sample rsyncs to one
depends_on: rank 3 removes most of the per-trip dial cost — do this only if the engine flip stalls, or for throttled-cluster resilience

ranked.name: Skip the reporter walk when announce evidence answers the lifecycle question (reconcile partial-announce, min_rows double-report)
ranked.cost: One 20-25 min walk per mid-flight reconcile on large runs (partial announce demoted to evidence while the walk still runs); min_rows gate runs the full reporter TWICE per aggregate (~10-60s, half pure duplication)
ranked.fix: Drop fut_status from the reconcile probe when any announcement exists (census counts + alive probe suffice mid-flight); have the reporter emit rows_observed per task in one pass so both min_rows sets compute locally from a single report

ranked.name: Parallel cold local content-hash (ThreadPool in _build_local_manifest_cached)
ranked.cost: MEASURED 37 min cold for 39,374 files / 9.9 GB (run-13); cache already kills the re-push case, this fixes the unavoidable cold/invalidation case: 4-8-way hashing ≈ 6-10 min
ranked.fix: Hash cache-miss files on a small ThreadPoolExecutor (hashlib releases the GIL; entries sorted after, cache write identical)

ranked.name: Poll-loop constant hygiene: transient faults sleep the floor not the backed-off interval; poll_until_terminal local default 30s -> event-driven/2s; batched-tick single announce exec
ranked.cost: Up to 300s extra staleness per transient blip while backed off; up to 30s dead air per local rendezvous; one extra cold dial per census tick when wave-combining (240 avoidable dials over a 4h watch)
ranked.fix: Reset backoff on transient faults and sleep the floor; rendezvous via wait-detached pid + one journal read; read_announcements returns marker FILENAMES so counts + complete-set derive from one exec (delete _census_complete_task_ids)

ranked.name: Event-driven terminal notice: remote long-poll on the announce dir replaces the client sleep
ranked.cost: Up to 300s (expected ~150s at cap) added to every quiet run's time-to-notice-terminal by the adaptive backoff
ranked.fix: One bounded ssh exec waiting server-side (until-loop on marker count, deadline = current effective interval, ack-gated); terminal marker wakes the client within ~1s; backoff then governs only the exec's server-side deadline
depends_on: rank 3 (a persistent channel makes a held long-poll session cheap; without it this holds one of 2 slots)
rank 6 (announce dir must exist from tick 1)

ranked.name: Fleet-level census: one exec per login node listing all runs' announce dirs
ranked.cost: N watched runs = N-2N dials/interval through the 2-slot limiter; slot-wait timeout worst case ~7 min for one tick; burst risk against MaxStartups
ranked.fix: Single ssh exec listing .hpc/announce/*/ (batch_status pattern applied to the announce census) fanned into every run's journal; per-run watchers read the shared local snapshot
depends_on: rank 3 substantially reduces the pressure (8 sessions per slot) — re-measure after the engine flip before spending L effort here

ranked.name: Engine idle-recycle horizon aware of registered poll cadences (IDLE_CLOSE_SEC=120 vs 300s backoff cap)
ranked.cost: Post-engine-flip only: ~1-3s reconnect per poll for any loop whose interval exceeds 120s (~48 reconnects ≈ 1-2.5 min over a 4h idle watch, each a fresh open the ban filter counts)
ranked.fix: Watch loops declare their cadence (next_tick_seconds already computed); engine keeps connections whose next expected use is scheduled, or IDLE_CLOSE_SEC = max(120, active-loop interval + margin)
depends_on: rank 3 (only exists once the engine is default)

ranked.name: Failure-path hygiene: tighter connect-failure ladder (2x15s not 3-5x60s) + rsync exec-ENOENT classified non-retryable with explicit -e native-ssh
ranked.cost: 3.2-5.5 min per dead-host first-detection (5x60s+sleeps); rsync ENOENT ladder measured >=30s x3 identical workers (mostly historical since rsync left PATH, misclassification persists)
ranked.fix: Distinct connect-failure schedule (ConnectTimeout=15s already fail-fasts; command budget untouched); classify exec-ENOENT/exit-12 retry_safe:false surfacing remediation; pass -e <native OpenSSH path> on win32

dedup_notes: DROPPED F-1 (_aggregated ships in deploy payload): VERIFIED already fixed at HEAD — _excludes.py:56-96 PROTECTED_OUTPUT_DIRS contains _aggregated/, _per_task_results/, _per_task_traces/, _dossier/ (comments cite F10 and run-13 finding 4; pinned by tests/infra/test_pull_dest_excludes.py). The evidence miner's ~10-min transfer + scan-inflation measurements predate the fix; its residual (remote manifest checkpoint) is the same object as A-4 and folds into rank 5.

B-1 = F-2: same root cause (deterministic reporter error rc=2 classified transient; only rc 126/127 escalate — verified at verify_canary.py:91,109-111). Merged into rank 1 with the sidecar-shipping root fix.

B-5 + E-6 + F-4 + F-7 are one root cause: heavyweight per-poll remote reporter (login shell + conda activation + 2 python starts + full task walk) that also feeds false failures to the breaker under 60s timeouts and fork exhaustion. Merged into rank 4.
D-1 + D-2 + F-6 are one fix (the tar pull engine with server-side filtering, delta, batched checkpoints, stall-based deadline); D-3 (second trace pull) falls out for free — merged into rank 2.
A-4 = F-8 residual (remote full-tree re-hash per push) — rank 5.
E-1, E-7, E-8, E-11, F-3 are all consequences of the one-shot cold-dial default; E-2 (asyncssh engine default-on) subsumes them — rank 3. E-10 becomes relevant only after it (rank 24).
A-7 = E-3 (submit-chain round-trip fan) and B-4 = E-4 (verify-tail fan): identical findings from two censuses — merged into rank 18, value substantially reduced by rank 3.
C-6 = D-5: per-wave combine exec class found on both the monitor and aggregate sides — merged into rank 16.

A-2 = D-8: same subprocess-fan-of-same-package-primitives class (submit-preflight, aggregate-preflight, block-drive spans) — merged into rank 12; A-1 (fast-path disqualification) kept separate as rank 13 since the MCP/CLI entry process pays it regardless.
C-2 (two execs per census tick) subsumed by the census fold — grouped into rank 21's hygiene batch; rank 23 (fleet census) subsumes it further at fleet scale.
F-2's fix ('escalate after scheduler shows terminal') and B-1's fix ('structured error codes first-class') combined: both layers kept in rank 1.

Excluded per evidence: F-9 largely historical (rsync off PATH) — kept only as the misclassification residual in rank 25. Run-12's KeyError 'estimator' crash flagged by the miner is a code bug already in the post-Jul-10 fix window, not a standing latency source — not ranked.
synthesis.totals: Typical healthy submit->canary->fleet->harvest->aggregate cycle: ~60-120 min saved. Breakdown: canary stage ~30-40 min (rank 1 removes the measured 30-min rc=2 spin; rank 8 removes the ~3-10 min serial second cycle); harvest/aggregate ~20-60 min (rank 2 converts the measured 40-90-min pull class to seconds-to-minutes; ranks 9/11/16/19 remove partial pulls, whole-root pulls, per-wave dials, and duplicate reporter walks); S2 push ~15-25 min (rank 5 removes the measured ~12-min remote re-hash, rank 7 saves ~5-7 min on GB-scale transfers, rank 15 saves 0.5-2.5 min of walks; the already-landed _aggregated exclude and local hash cache banked the rest); SSH substrate + dispatch ~5-15 min (rank 3: 2-6 min handshake + ~9 min breaker trips when they fire; ranks 12-14: 1-4 min of interpreter cold starts); monitor time-to-terminal-notice 2-5 min (ranks 4/6/21/22). Failure-shaped nights compound far larger: run-12's 5.7 h aggregate night collapses to <1 h under ranks 2+17, and the 11-h dead-watch window under rank 4. Re-submit within the canary TTL saves a further 5-20 min if rank 10's ruling lands.
missed: Cluster-side environment skew as a latency ROOT (the python 3.11->3.13 module-reload warning behind every run-13 sidecar_not_found): no census audited a doctor/preflight check that would catch reporter-env skew BEFORE a 30-min canary spin — rank 1 shortens the detection but nothing prevents the broken deploy.

Canary scheduler placement: no census examined requesting a debug/short/express partition or QOS for canary jobs — queue wait was scoped out, but the canary's queue wait is a spec choice, not scheduler fate, and it is doubled by the n=2 prior.

LLM-turn and harness latency between block boundaries: time the driving agent spends composing/relaying between next_block hops (and MCP round-trip overhead) was in nobody's scope; on relay-driven runs it can rival the mechanical costs.

Windows Defender / antivirus overhead on the 39k-file NTFS enumerations and hash scans — the 37-min cold hash and 10-40s walks were measured but never attributed between IO and AV interception; an exclusion for the experiment tree could be a free multiplier on ranks 15/20.
The wheel build + three-env reinstall loop (release skill, uv build, cluster env refresh) — a recurring multi-minute wall-clock cost between fix and next run, unaudited.
Detached-worker spawn cost on Windows (process creation + hook env inheritance) and the journal/JSONL growth curve on long campaigns (append/read costs as ledgers grow) — both unmeasured.

Alternative bulk-transfer planes: nobody evaluated routing GB-scale pulls/pushes via the cluster's DTN/scratch staging or compressing on the remote side before transfer — rank 2/7 optimize the existing VPN path (~1-2 MB/s) rather than questioning it.
MCP server process lifetime: whether mcp-serve keeps a warm registry (avoiding the 7.2s walk per tool call) or re-pays dispatch per call was not explicitly measured by any census.
Local combiner/reduce CPU time for large aggregates (the reduce itself, post-pull) — all censuses audited transfer and round-trips, none profiled the local reduce/dedup pass at 2700-task scale.
